operation: First, a 20 mm × 9 mm × 2 mm rectangular box is created on the XY workplane. All edges parallel to the Z axis are filleted with a 4 mm radius, followed by filleting all edges parallel to the X axis with a 0.5 mm radius. A new workplane is established on the top (positive Z) face of the base, with its center offset 5.5 mm along the X axis. Two concentric circles, 4.5 mm (outer) and 2.3 mm (inner) in radius, are drawn on this workplane, then extruded 7 mm upwards along the Z axis to form a hollow cylindrical boss. Finally, the top edges of the extruded cylindrical feature are filleted with a 0.2 mm radius.
geometry: The model is a monolithic solid composed of a thin, oblong rounded rectangular base and an offset hollow vertical cylinder affixed to one side of the base's upper surface. The ends of the oblong base have large rounded corners, while the thinner vertical edges of the base have smaller fillets. The hollow cylindrical feature has a concentric through-hole running its full height, and its top circumferential edge is lightly rounded.
function: This is a compact standoff mounting bracket, designed to be secured via its flat base to a mounting surface, with the central hollow cylindrical through-hole accepting a fastener, pin, or shaft to provide an offset, raised anchor or alignment point for mechanical assembly.
